courses.subject: MEDM
courses.title: General Endocrinology and Metabolism II
courses.description:
• Pathophysiology of the endocrine system and principles underlying the maintenance of homeostasis in human body
• The effects of environment and lifestyles on mental and physical health and the neurohormonal and metabolic basis for the clinical manifestation of diseases
• Clinical approach including diagnosis, assessment and management of patients with endocrine and metabolic diseases
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Endocrinology, Diabetes & Metabolism
courses.learning_outcomes:
• Overview of the interconnectivity of different pathways within the neuroendocrine system with diverse clinical presentations
• Know the clinical features and basic principles for management of adrenal, growth and reproductive disorders including special needs in children and pregnant women
• Understand the hormonal responses and pitfalls in data interpretation and hormonal replacement during critical illnesses
• Recognize the importance of using a multidisciplinary, multi-method and multi-staged approach to diagnose and localize an endocrine disorder as well as the short and long term management including monitoring and hormone replacement
courses.course_syllabus:
• 12 lectures in 4 seminars and 3 interactive workshops explaining the epidemiology, clinical presentation, investigations and management of various endocrine disorders including adrenal glands, growth and reproduction
• 5 clinical attachments including ward rounds and outpatient clinic visits to appreciate the clinical diversity of endocrine disorders

courses.subject: MEDM
courses.title: Obesity and Weight Management
courses.description:
• Overview of epidemiology, medical hazards and management of obesity with particular emphasis on application of knowledge to implement multidisciplinary care
• Interplay between neurohormonal regulation and cognitive- psychological-behavioral abnormalities in obesity
• The assessments and inter-dependence between body composition, physical fitness and metabolic rate
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Endocrinology, Diabetes & Metabolism
courses.learning_outcomes:
• Learn the epidemiology, classification and multiple morbidities associated with obesity
• Understand the scientific principles underlying regulation of energy metabolism by multiple organs including brain, gut, liver, muscle, pancreas and adipose tissues
• Recognize the past, present and future strategies for weight management including pharmacological, non-pharmacological and behavioral interventions
• Appreciate the evidence base and utility of different diets and exercise programs in weight management
• Know the latest recommendations and controversies regarding surgical treatment for obesity and after care
courses.course_syllabus:
• 2 seminars with 6 lectures illustrating the principles of diagnosis, pathophysiology and treatment of obesity
• 2 interactive workshops illustrating the practical management of obesity including exercise, diet, drugs, surgery and behavioral therapy

courses.subject: MEDM
courses.title: General Endocrinology and Metabolism III
courses.description:
• Pathophysiology of the endocrine system and principles underlying the maintenance of homeostasis in human body
• The effects of environment and lifestyles on mental and physical health and the neurohormonal and metabolic basis for the clinical manifestation of diseases
• Clinical approach including diagnosis, assessment and management of patients with endocrine and metabolic diseases
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MSc Endocrinology, Diabetes & Metabolism
courses.learning_outcomes:
• Understand the mechanisms underlying common often symptomatic endocrine/metabolic disorders manifested as electrolyte and lipid abnormalities
• Recognize endocrine causes for abnormal growth and reproduction as well as dysregulation of sex hormones and how to order investigations and interpret results for appropriate clinical decisions
courses.course_syllabus:
• 2 interactive workshops explaining the regulation of electrolyte control and lipid metabolism as well as the regulation of growth hormone and sex steroids in determining growth and reproduction
• 1 clinical attachment to combined clinic to appreciate the clinical presentation of common gynecological and growth problems
